prompt: Create a feature spec for adding a notifications system to TaskFlow. Users should get notified when: assigned a task, watched task status changes, created task completed, someone comments on their task. In-app only v1 — bell icon, dropdown, mark read. Backend: notifications table, REST endpoints, emit from existing task handlers. NOT doing: email, push, websockets, preferences.

assertions.name: has_all_sections
assertions.description: Spec contains all required sections from the template

assertions.name: problem_explains_why
assertions.criteria: The Problem section explains WHY notifications matter (user impact, workflow disruption) — not just 'we need notifications'
assertions.description: Problem section articulates motivation and impact

assertions.name: scope_has_nongoals
assertions.description: Out of Scope section exists and mentions key exclusions

assertions.name: references_real_files
assertions.criteria: Technical Approach references at least 2 real files from the codebase (e.g., taskStore.ts, tasks.ts, Sidebar, TaskCard, types.ts)
assertions.description: References actual codebase files, not hypothetical ones

assertions.name: makes_architectural_decisions
assertions.criteria: The spec makes concrete decisions about: how notifications are fetched (polling interval or on-demand), where notification state lives (Zustand store or React Query), and how backend emits notifications (inline in handlers vs event system). Decisions are stated, not left as open questions.
assertions.description: Key architectural decisions are made, not punted

assertions.name: verification_is_testable
assertions.criteria: Verification section has specific testable behaviors (e.g., 'bell icon shows unread count', 'clicking notification navigates to task') — not vague items like 'notifications work correctly'
assertions.description: Verification items are concrete and testable

assertions.name: has_alternatives
assertions.criteria: Alternatives Considered section has at least one real alternative approach (not just 'do nothing') with reasoning for rejection
assertions.description: At least one substantive alternative considered

assertions.name: no_line_numbers
assertions.description: No exact line number references like file.tsx:123

assertions.name: no_code_snippets
assertions.description: No TypeScript/JS code blocks in the spec

assertions.name: no_json_shapes
assertions.criteria: The spec does NOT contain verbatim JSON payload definitions, interface definitions, or schema shapes. Describing what data is needed is fine; writing out the exact JSON structure is over-specification.

assertions.name: no_mechanism_tables
assertions.criteria: The spec does NOT contain exhaustive tables mapping every feature/tool to its exact implementation mechanism, handler, or infrastructure. A summary table of features in scope is fine; a table prescribing exact function names and wiring is over-specification.

assertions.name: no_redundant_subsections
assertions.criteria: Technical Approach does NOT have separate Key Modules and Approach subsections that restate the same file-to-role mappings. File references should be woven into the approach prose, not listed as a separate manifest. If you can draw a 1:1 mapping between a Key Modules bullet and an Approach paragraph, that is redundancy.
assertions.description: Technical Approach does not triple-tell the same info across subsections

assertions.name: decision_density
assertions.criteria: Count distinct non-obvious architectural decisions stated in the Technical Approach section. A decision is: choosing between two valid alternatives (e.g., polling vs websocket, Zustand vs context, server-owned vs client-inferred). Restating scope or describing how existing code works does NOT count. The spec should contain at least 5 distinct decisions.
assertions.description: At least 5 distinct architectural decisions in Technical Approach

assertions.name: no_obvious_failure_modes
assertions.criteria: Failure Modes section only lists scenarios where the RECOVERY STRATEGY is a non-obvious design decision. Standard error handling (return 404 on not-found, validate auth, return error on bad input) should NOT appear. Every failure mode entry should make the reader think "oh, I wouldn't have assumed that recovery strategy."
assertions.description: Failure modes contain only non-obvious recovery strategies
